name: F1-stake-collusion
notes: Acceptable-damage fixture. Two validators holding 90% combined stake collude to award high score to miner M-2's fabricated submission. Honest validator V-3 (10% stake) reruns and rejects M-2 in its own deterministic core — but YUMA consensus follows the 90% stake bloc. The fixture documents the actual residual damage: M-2's fraudulent settlement reaches `settled-supported` despite V-3's correct rejection. The defence in this case is HM-REQ-0070 two-tier settlement: the deferred 30% can claw back via T-OVR if a fresh honest submission later flips the result. T-015 (cross-validator anomaly detection) is partial; closing it is on the Phase 2+ roadmap.

validators.comment: votes M-2's submission as supported regardless of artifact integrity; skips own rerun
runs the deterministic core including rerun; correctly scores M-2 as zero on reproduction component

submission.comment: ran one seed, signed a manifest claiming five; honest rerun catches this on the seeds it samples

inputs.rerun_seed_for_V3: deterministic in (V-3 hotkey, epoch=1, H-0001, v=1) -> samples [1, 3]; V-3 finds metrics differ from M-2's declarations

settlements.comment: YUMA consensus follows 90% stake. M-2's submission settles even though V-3 correctly scores it zero. The 30% deferred portion (HM-REQ-0070) is the only remaining recourse and only fires if a fresh honest submission flips the result within the 6-month window via T-OVR.

rejections.comment: V-3's deterministic core correctly fires HM-REQ-0010 reproduction-failure; but V-3 is one validator in the YUMA aggregation, not the aggregator

validator_penalties.comment: V-3 is the lone outlier; YUMA down-weights V-3's stake share in the next epoch. This is the hole F1 documents and the design has not yet fully closed; T-015 (cross-validator anomaly detection) is partial and will be implemented in Phase 2+.
V-1 and V-2 agree with each other; YUMA cannot distinguish their lie from honest consensus when they ARE the consensus

expected_outcome.cost_comment: The colluding bloc's cost is the stake they already hold (a sunk cost of subnet participation, not a per-attack cost). The design does not price this out; HM-REQ-0070 deferred-settlement is the only economic disincentive (lose the 30% if T-OVR fires within 6 months).
